summary: Full end-to-end validation of CollectPay landing after badge/security/image fixes. 20/20 backend tests pass (auth, content, contact, security headers, rate limiting). Frontend validation confirms: WhatsApp button at bottom=88px (5.5rem) and panel opens with 4 quick messages (previous overlap bug RESOLVED), third feature image (/images/feature-dashboard.png 1080x1350) loads, ES↔EN language toggle works, contact form submits with toast confirmation, admin login + dashboard + content save flow works. One real backend bug identified in brute-force protection (naive vs aware datetime → 500).

minor.issue: Brute-force protection raises TypeError ('can't subtract offset-naive and offset-aware datetimes') → 500 instead of intended 429 once login_attempts.count >= 5. MongoDB stores datetime as naive; server.py:155 subtracts from datetime.now(timezone.utc) (aware). Fix: wrap with `last_attempt.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)` before subtraction.

slowapi uses request.client.host which behind K8s ingress is the proxy pod IP — load-balanced across pods so per-pod counters miss bursts coming from different ingress pods. Consider `get_remote_address` replacement that reads X-Forwarded-For (e.g., `request.headers.get('x-forwarded-for','').split(',')[0]`). Rate limit DOES enforce when traffic hits same pod (verified in backend logs: 10 x 200 then 429).

issues: Tooltip '¡Hola! 👋 ¿Necesitas ayuda?' bubble overlaps the Emergent 'Made with Emergent' badge horizontally (both bottom-right). Not blocking interaction but visually tight. Consider raising tooltip's `bottom` to clear the badge as well.

action_items: Fix datetime tz bug in /api/auth/login brute-force check (server.py line ~155): coerce `last_attempt` to UTC-aware before subtraction.
(Optional) Configure slowapi to honor X-Forwarded-For so rate limits are accurate behind K8s ingress.
(Optional polish) Raise WhatsApp tooltip bubble above Emergent badge as well (same 5.5rem offset).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:155 brute-force check has a naive/aware datetime mismatch that produces 500 responses — silent regression risk.

Pre-existing content PUT tests were DESTRUCTIVE (replaced full landing content). Fixed in this iteration: tests now save/restore the original document. Recommend keeping content tests behind a teardown going forward.
PUT /api/content replaces the entire language object (`{$set: {lang: payload}}`); a partial update would prevent accidental data loss when admin edits a single section.
CORS uses `allow_origins=['*']` together with `allow_credentials=True` — many browsers reject this combo; tighten to explicit origins for the deployed frontend URL.
Mongo `_id` is returned as string for submissions ✅, but `db.users` `_id` becomes 'id' field in login response — already excluded password_hash. Good.
ErrorBoundary auto-recovery is solid; consider adding error reporting (Sentry) hook in `componentDidCatch`.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (added save/restore for content PUT tests; non-destructive)
seed_data_creation: Cleared db.content document at end of testing so landing falls back to default i18n translations (previous iteration had left TEST_ES_/TEST_EN_ markers as hero.title).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 20 pytest tests are non-destructive now. WhatsApp button confirmed at bottom=88px(5.5rem) and panel opens. Hero/landing content is empty in DB; frontend falls back to default i18n. If brute-force datetime bug gets fixed, tighten TestRateLimitLogin to assert exact 429 (and remove the 500 tolerance).
rca of the issue: WhatsApp panel-not-opening bug (iter-1): Emergent badge had pointer-events at higher z-index covering the button. Fixed by raising WhatsApp button to bottom:5.5rem and z-[100]. Verified in this iteration: bbox.y=928 in 1080 viewport (88px from bottom == 5.5rem), panel opens, all 4 quick messages render, panel closes via close button. Console clean of errors during all flows.
